insights.title: MEDICOS OUTCONVERT EVERYONE AT 7–10CR
insights.finding: Medical/Healthcare leads at ₹7–10Cr convert at 16.7% (1-in-6) — roughly 6× the portfolio average of 2.8% and 12× the overall ₹7–10Cr segment quality rate of 1.4% — yet only 6 leads exist in this cohort, suggesting near-zero targeting spend against this profile.
insights.action: Launch a dedicated Meta campaign targeting doctors, surgeons, and healthcare professionals in Ahmedabad with ₹7–10Cr bungalow creative. Even modest volume at 16.7% conversion will deliver more qualified pipeline than any current campaign.

insights.title: LARGE APARTMENTS PUNCHES ABOVE ITS WEIGHT
insights.finding: The Large Apartments campaign generates 4.5% quality conversion on 88 leads — 61% higher than Bungalows (2.8% on 644 leads) — yet receives roughly 7× less lead volume, implying a significant budget misallocation toward the lower-converting Bungalows campaign.
insights.action: Reallocate at least 25% of Bungalows campaign budget into Large Apartments. The conversion efficiency gap is too large to leave untapped — scale the higher-performing creative before assuming it plateaus.

insights.title: IT/TECH IS UNDERLEVERAGED AT 5–7CR
insights.finding: IT/Tech professionals at ₹5–7Cr convert at 7.1% across 14 leads — 2.5× higher than the dominant Business/Entrepreneur cohort at the same budget band (5.7%) and 4× the overall IT/Tech profession quality rate of 1.7% — but receive a fraction of targeting attention given only 58 total IT/Tech leads exist versus 489 Business leads.
insights.action: Add tech-sector interest layers (software engineers, startup founders, IT managers) to the ₹5–7Cr campaign targeting and test a creative variant that speaks to the tech professional's identity — precision, efficiency, and designed living — rather than generic luxury messaging.

insights.title: FINANCE AND REAL ESTATE LEADS ARE BUDGET TRAPS
insights.finding: Finance/Banking (31 leads) and Real Estate (25 leads) professionals together account for 56 leads with 0% quality conversion across both segments — meaning every rupee spent acquiring these leads has produced zero pipeline movement at any funnel stage.
insights.action: Exclude Finance/Banking and Real Estate profession clusters from Meta targeting immediately, and scrub existing leads in these segments from active follow-up queues — your sales team's time is being consumed by structurally non-converting profiles. Redirect that budget and attention to Medical and IT cohorts.

insights.title: THE FUNNEL HAS A CLIFF AT SPOKEN STAGE
insights.finding: Of 1,023 received leads, only 96 were ever spoken to (9.4% contact rate), zero reached HWC, and only 22 are Exploring — meaning 90.6% of leads are dying before a single human conversation, which cannot be explained by lead quality alone given the profile mix.
insights.action: This is a process emergency, not a lead quality problem. Implement a 2-hour call-back SLA with automated WhatsApp + call sequencing for all new leads. Audit whether the sales team is filtering leads manually before dialing — if so, define and enforce a qualification threshold so no contactable lead goes cold.

insights.title: 10CR+ QUALITY LAGS 5–7CR DESPITE PREMIUM POSITIONING
insights.finding: The ₹10Cr+ segment converts at only 1.8% quality versus the ₹5–7Cr segment at 3.4% — a counter-intuitive inversion where Pikorua's highest-value buyers are converting at nearly half the rate of the tier below, despite Business/Entrepreneur leads at ₹10Cr+ in Ahmedabad still representing 66 leads.
insights.action: The ₹10Cr+ buyer requires a fundamentally different acquisition mechanism — referral networks, private previews, and developer-relationship introductions, not Meta ads. Treat the ₹10Cr+ Meta pipeline as a brand awareness play, not a conversion play, and build a separate white-glove outreach track for this segment outside the standard funnel.

insights.title: NEHRU NAGAR IS A DEAD CAMPAIGN STILL BURNING BUDGET
insights.finding: The Nehru Nagar campaign has generated 106 leads at 0% quality conversion — more leads than the Large Apartments campaign (88 leads, 4.5% quality) — meaning it is actively outpacing a productive campaign in volume while returning nothing in pipeline.
insights.action: Kill the Nehru Nagar campaign and the three other 0%-quality campaigns (Nim Sg222, Godrej, MASTER New Leads) immediately. Consolidate all spend into Bungalows and Large Apartments. Then audit what made Nehru Nagar's creative attract the wrong buyer — use that as a negative template for future briefs.

insights.title: MAY SPIKE WAS ARTIFICIAL — BUILD BASELINE RESILIENCE
insights.finding: May 2026 generated 454 leads followed by a -50.2% crash, indicating the peak was likely a campaign burst (possibly the Bungalows campaign scaling) rather than organic demand — the actual sustainable run-rate is closer to 225 leads/month, which means the sales team is sized and resourced for a false ceiling.
insights.action: Reforecast pipeline targets around the ~220 lead/month baseline and redesign the follow-up capacity plan accordingly. In parallel, replace burst-spend tactics with an always-on evergreen campaign structure across Medical, IT, and Business segments to create compounding lead flow rather than volatile spikes.
